Institutional Orphans.desc: Zero sell-side coverage. Insider-controlled floats too small for funds to touch. Priced where no one is looking — and frequently at cash or below book.

Hidden Compounders.desc: Quality franchises earning high returns on capital, but mispriced due to segment complexity, recent spinouts, or transitional optics. See's Candies economics at panic-price multiples.

Activist Situations.desc: Credentialed activists in 13D-filed campaigns — operational turnarounds, capital-return programs, sales, and breakups. Hard catalysts with defined timelines.

Spinoffs.desc: Recent or pending spinoffs where forced index selling, management independence, and clean balance sheets create the Greenblatt setup.

Merger Arbitrage.desc: Announced deals, tender offers, self-tenders, take-privates, and litigation-resolution arbs. Short-duration, defined-risk, defined-upside situations.

Holding Company Discounts.desc: Holding companies trading at persistent discounts to NAV. Family-controlled compounders, asset-rich structures, and hidden-asset conglomerates.

Cyclicals & CEFs.desc: Cyclical-trough valuations at survivable balance sheets, plus closed-end funds trading at wide, persistent discounts with activist pressure or capital-return programs.

Distressed & Binary.desc: Post-bankruptcy equities, distressed debt-to-equity situations, FDA and biotech binaries, SPAC liquidations, rights offerings, and reverse-split survivors. Higher variance, higher reward.
